# Private Inference for Agents ÔÇö Live Status

**Hermes wraps every outgoing chat request in an httpx transport that encrypts the conversation to a CVM-attested public key.** Only a TEE-verified GPU running the named model can decrypt it.

> **This page is a workflow artifact, not a hand-written doc.**
> Regenerated by [`tee-attestation-report.yml`](../.github/workflows/tee-attestation-report.yml) on a [scheduled cron](../.github/workflows/tee-attestation-report.yml#L5) (13:17 UTC daily) and on every push that touches the transport or verifier. Each run re-executes [`scripts/attestation_report.py`](../scripts/attestation_report.py) against live providers and commits the result back to the branch ÔÇö the table below is whatever the most recent run observed.

[![TEE Attestation](https://github.com/amiller/hermes-agent/actions/workflows/tee-attestation-report.yml/badge.svg?branch=feat/near-ai-attestation)](https://github.com/amiller/hermes-agent/actions/workflows/tee-attestation-report.yml)

## Upstream integration

- **[NousResearch/hermes-agent#12201](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/pull/12201)** ÔÇö Add NEAR AI + RedPill TDX attestation and the in-process E2EE httpx transport. Branch `feat/near-ai-attestation`.
- Transport lives at [`hermes_cli/e2ee_transport.py`](../hermes_cli/e2ee_transport.py) ┬Ě verifier at [`hermes_cli/attestation.py`](../hermes_cli/attestation.py) ┬Ě script at [`scripts/attestation_report.py`](../scripts/attestation_report.py).

## What this proves

Modern agents leak their full context ÔÇö memory, credentials, intermediate reasoning, tool outputs ÔÇö to whichever model they call. This workflow verifies, on every run, that the models Hermes talks to are running inside Intel TDX + NVIDIA confidential-compute enclaves and that the CLI's encryption key is bound to those specific enclaves.

If a row below is ÔŁî, the CLI refuses to send plaintext to that model and the `/model` picker hides it. No fallbacks.

---

## What's verified per row

1. Fetch the provider's live attestation bundle (`GET /v1/attestation/report?model=ÔÇŽ&nonce=ÔÇŽ`).
2. TDX quote cryptographically verified via Phala's verifier API.
3. `report_data` binds the CVM's signing address and our fresh nonce.
4. NVIDIA GPU attested via NRAS (`/v3/attest/gpu`).
5. `signing_public_key` derives to `signing_address` (keccak of pubkey last 20 bytes).

A Ôťů means all five held **at this run's timestamp**. The CLI wraps outgoing chat content in an httpx transport that encrypts to that `signing_public_key`, so only the attested model sees the plaintext.
